vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1053

plan.title: Consumer swarm fails on greenfield projects — no dispatch mechanism, bootstrap gap, PS 5.1 exit codes

narratives.Description: Consumer swarm fails on greenfield projects — no dispatch mechanism, bootstrap gap, PS 5.1 exit codes
narratives.Overview:
## Context

First-time consumer attempted to run deft-directive-swarm on a brand new Windows project (AnyoneCanMakeMusic). The swarm skill was triggered after deft-directive-setup completed Phases 1-3 (USER.md existed, PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json and scope vBRIEFs created in the same session).

Environment: Windows 11, PowerShell 5.1, Warp terminal, \task 3.48.0, uv 0.11.8, gh authenticated.

## Failures

### 1. No agent dispatch mechanism (critical)

start_agent was not available in the Warp tool set. The skill's fallback (Phase 3 Step 2b) asks the user to manually open N Warp tabs and paste prompts. This is not orchestration — it's a manual worksheet. The user asked 'why can't you just do it yourself?' and the agent had to abandon the swarm model and work serially.

**Impact**: The entire value proposition of the swarm skill (parallel execution) is lost without start_agent. The worktrees, branch naming conventions, and generated prompt files become dead artifacts.

**Suggestion**: Add a Phase 3 Step 2d fallback for single-agent serial execution when no dispatch mechanism is available. The agent should be able to execute worktree prompts sequentially itself rather than generating files nobody will consume.

### 2. Greenfield project bootstrap gap (high)

The swarm skill assumes git repo, GitHub remote, Taskfile integration, and deft task commands are all functional. A greenfield project created moments earlier by deft-directive-setup had none of this. The agent had to improvise:
- git init
- gh repo create
- Author a wrapper Taskfile.yml with includes: deft: ...
- Rename directive/ to deft/ (clone naming mismatch)
- Remove embedded .git from the cloned deft directory
- Add .worktrees/ and .venv/ to .gitignore

None of this is documented in the swarm skill or the setup skill. There's a gap between 'setup complete' and 'swarm-ready'.

**Suggestion**: Either (a) add a Phase 0 Step -1 bootstrap check to the swarm skill that detects and offers to fix missing infrastructure, or (b) extend deft-directive-setup Phase 2 to handle git/GitHub/Taskfile scaffolding as part of project configuration.

### 3. deft consumer installation friction (medium)

No automated install step for consumer projects. The agent had to:
- Clone deftai/directive manually
- Rename directive/ to deft/ (AGENTS.md says 'Install-generated AGENTS.md uses deft/-prefixed paths' but there's no install that does this)
- Remove .git from the clone (nested git repo warning)
- Create a wrapper Taskfile.yml with the include pattern
- Use \task -t ./deft/Taskfile.yml <command> fallback for every deft task

The \task install target exists but is described as 'dev convenience — end users should use the compiled binary from GitHub Releases'. No compiled binary or consumer install flow was found.

**Suggestion**: Ship a consumer install command (deft init or \task install:consumer) that handles clone → rename → .git removal → Taskfile include generation → AGENTS.md creation in one step.

### 4. PowerShell 5.1 exit code masking (medium)

Every \task scope:promote, scope:activate, and \vbrief:preflight call returned exit code 1 in PowerShell 5.1 despite succeeding. PS 5.1 treats any stderr output as a native command error, and the \task runner writes progress/info messages to stderr. This makes it impossible to programmatically distinguish success from failure.

The existing #798 rule covers UTF-8 encoding but not exit code masking.

**Suggestion**: Either (a) suppress stderr info messages in the Python lifecycle scripts when stdout is not a TTY, or (b) document the PS 5.1 exit code masking behavior and provide a workaround pattern (e.g., check for specific success strings in stdout rather than relying on exit codes).

### 5. Worktree placement guidance missing (low)

The skill's Phase 2 Step 1 example uses E:\Repos\deft-agent1 but provides no guidance on where worktrees should live relative to the project. The agent placed them as siblings (AnyoneCanMakeMusic-agent1/) which cluttered the user's Projects directory. Had to redo inside .worktrees/ with a .gitignore entry.

**Suggestion**: Recommend .worktrees/ inside the project root as the default pattern and add it to the generated .gitignore.

### 6. Lifecycle bridge overhead on fresh projects (low)

Running scope:promote then scope:activate on 4 vBRIEFs the agent created 5 minutes earlier — with explicit approval gates — was pure ceremony. The setup skill deposits in proposed/ and the swarm requires ^Gctive/; for greenfield projects in the same session, this round-trip adds friction with no safety value.

**Suggestion**: When the swarm is invoked in the same session that created the scope vBRIEFs (detectable via session continuity or vBRIEF creation timestamp), offer a fast-path that promotes+activates all in-scope candidates in one batch with a single confirmation.

## Environment

- Windows 11, PowerShell 5.1.26100.7705
- Warp terminal (no start_agent in tool set)
- deft cloned from deftai/directive (vendored, not submodule)
- task 3.48.0, uv 0.11.8, gh CLI authenticated
- Consumer project: https://github.com/Shinran7/AnyoneCanMakeMusic

references.title: Issue #1053: Consumer swarm fails on greenfield projects — no dispatch mechanism, bootstrap gap, PS 5.1 exit codes
Generic-terminal swarm fallback should offer serial self-execution instead of only manual prompt paste
Greenfield setup should produce or offer a swarm-ready project bootstrap
Lifecycle Taskfile commands should preserve reliable success semantics under Windows PowerShell 5.1
Interactive swarm worktrees should default to deterministic non-cluttering scratch paths
